instruction:
Add comprehensive edge case tests for `boost_tree`.


Existing tests for reference:
```r

```
```r
expect_snapshot(error = TRUE, boost_tree(mode = "bogus"))
```
```r
expect_snapshot(translate(boost_tree(mode = "classification"), engine = NULL))
```


The existing tests cover basic functionality. Add tests for:
1. NULL inputs to each argument
2. Empty vectors/strings: character(0), "", integer(0)
3. Boundary values: 0, 1, -1, NA, NaN, Inf
4. Invalid input types (passing numeric where character expected, etc.)
5. Missing arguments (use expect_error with class matching)
6. Very long inputs (strings > 1000 chars, vectors > 10000 elements)
context:
Package: parsnip v1.4.1.9002
Description: A Common API to Modeling and Analysis Functions
Function: boost_tree
Parameters: mode, engine, mtry, trees, min_n, tree_depth, learn_rate, loss_reduction, sample_size, stop_iter
Test patterns used: expect_snapshot, test_that
Expectations used: expect_no_error, expect_snapshot

Function Code:
```r
boost_tree <-
  function(
    mode = "unknown",
    engine = "xgboost",
    mtry = NULL,
    trees = NULL,
    min_n = NULL,
    tree_depth = NULL,
    learn_rate = NULL,
    loss_reduction = NULL,
    sample_size = NULL,
    stop_iter = NULL
  ) {
    args <- list(
      mtry = enquo(mtry),
      trees = enquo(trees),
      min_n = enquo(min_n),
      tree_depth = enquo(tree_depth),
      learn_rate = enquo(learn_rate),
      loss_reduction = enquo(loss_reduction),
      sample_size = enquo(sample_size),
      stop_iter = enquo(stop_iter)
    )

    new_model_spec(
      "boost_tree",
      args,
      eng_args = NULL,
      mode = mode,
      user_specified_mode = !missing(mode),
      method = NULL,
      engine = engine,
      user_specified_engine = !missing(engine)
    )
  }
```
reference_test:
expect_snapshot(error = TRUE, boost_tree(mode = "bogus"))

expect_snapshot(translate(boost_tree(mode = "classification"), engine = NULL))
